opp.name: Whirlpool_S2P_2025
opp.next_step: June 17 Rick: No change. Project Delayed per champion, prospect shows pattern of starting/stopping their proc initiative - questionable funding
opp.ais_why:
• Project started in 2024 around Supplier, Sourcing, and Merlin — and grew into a full S2C effort to replace iValua due to gaps in AI, but momentum has now stalled.
• May 13 update is blunt: no change, project delayed per the champion, and the prospect shows a clear start-stop pattern on procurement initiatives.
• Last activity April 21, 2026 — Merlin being in scope keeps it Curious, but the stop-start pattern caps the score.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c: null
Next Step 2026-06-17: funding is questionable, no value case articulated
Zero Avoma calls with buyer (no discovery or value-building conversation recorded)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case (cost reduction, cycle time improvement, headcount savings, error-rate reduction, or ROI) is documented in any Salesforce field or buyer conversation. The buyer has not articulated measurable value drivers or financial impact. Without a quantified value case, Whirlpool cannot build an internal business case to secure funding or executive approval. The Next Step update (17 Jun) confirms funding is questionable, which directly correlates to the absence of a quantified ROI story that would justify the investment. The immediate move is to run a value discovery session with the Decision Makers (Priscila Coelho, ASHWINI N SHINGOTE) and the deal owner's manager to surface and quantify the pain (procurement cycle time, supplier risk exposure, contract leakage, manual invoice processing cost) and co-build the ROI model that unlocks budget.

sources: SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_Identified__c: null
SF OpportunityContactRole: no Champion role assigned
Next Step 2026-06-17: 'per champion' (unnamed, passive, reports delays but does not unblock)
Zero Avoma calls (no champion-building conversation recorded)
champion.narrative: No champion is formally identified (SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_Identified__c are null, and no contact role is marked Champion). The Next Step update (17 Jun) references 'per champion' when reporting the project delay and funding concerns, which implies someone at Whirlpool is providing internal updates to Rick Taranek, but this person is not named, their role is not stated, and there is zero evidence they are advocating for Zycus, introducing us to the Economic Buyer, or driving the evaluation forward. A true champion has access to power, actively sells Zycus internally, unblocks obstacles, and shapes the criteria in our favor. The unnamed champion in the Next Step is passive: they report delays but do not resolve them, they surface funding concerns but do not secure budget, and they allow the deal to stall for 283 days without escalating or re-engaging. This is a weak, non-advocate contact, not a champion. The deal is at serious risk without a strong, active champion who can navigate Whirlpool's internal politics, secure executive sponsorship, and restart momentum. The immediate move is to identify and develop a real champion, likely among the Decision Makers (Priscila Coelho, ASHWINI N SHINGOTE) or the Tech/Data/AI Strategy lead (Caio A Aggio), by running a value-discovery session, co-building the business case, and equipping them to sell Zycus internally.

sources: Next Step 2026-06-17: project delayed, funding questionable, pattern of starting/stopping procurement initiatives
SF Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c: null
Zero Avoma calls (no competitive discussion recorded)
Zero formal evaluation activity (no demo, RFI, RFP in 283 days)
competition.narrative: The primary competitor is Do Nothing / Deprioritization. The Next Step update (17 Jun) states the project is delayed, funding is questionable, and Whirlpool shows a pattern of starting and stopping procurement initiatives. This is not a competitive loss to another vendor; it is a loss to inertia, competing priorities, and weak internal sponsorship. The buyer is not actively evaluating alternatives (no RFP, no demo, no competing vendor named in any field or conversation). The risk is that Whirlpool indefinitely postpones the procurement transformation, continues with their current fragmented stack (likely manual processes, spreadsheets, legacy ERP procurement modules, or an incumbent vendor they are dissatisfied with but not urgent enough to replace), and never allocates budget. To win against Do Nothing, Zycus must create urgency by quantifying the cost of inaction (procurement cycle time, contract leakage, supplier risk, manual processing cost, missed savings), building a compelling ROI case, securing Economic Buyer sponsorship, and anchoring the timeline to a forcing function (fiscal year planning, compliance deadline, executive mandate). Without these, the deal remains stalled indefinitely.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c: null
Product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) implies broad procurement pain, but buyer has not confirmed it
Next Step 2026-06-17: project delayed, funding questionable (pain not validated or quantified)
Zero Avoma calls (no discovery conversation recorded)
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. The buyer has not stated cycle-time problems, supplier risk exposure, contract leakage, manual invoice processing cost, compliance gaps, or procurement visibility issues in any recorded conversation or Salesforce field. The product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) implies pain across contract management, sourcing, supplier collaboration, requisition-to-pay, and invoicing, but this is inferred from the solution scope, not confirmed by the buyer. Without confirmed pain, there is no urgency, no compelling event, and no clear pain owner driving the initiative internally. The Next Step update (17 Jun) confirms the project is delayed and funding is questionable, which correlates directly to the absence of a validated, quantified pain story. If the pain is not real or urgent to Whirlpool, the deal will remain stalled. The immediate move is to run a discovery session with the Decision Makers (Priscila Coelho, ASHWINI N SHINGOTE) and influencers (Caio A Aggio, Pravallika Yemba) to surface and confirm the pain, quantify the impact, and identify the pain owner (the person whose budget or performance is hurt by the status quo).

sources: SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c, Mandate__c: null
Zero Avoma calls (no contracting, legal, InfoSec, or implementation discussion recorded)
Stage: Qualified (no paper process initiated)
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec review, ERP integration scoping, procurement channel) have been discussed or documented. The deal is still in Qualified stage with no formal evaluation started, so paper process is premature. However, for a large enterprise procurement transformation at Whirlpool (a Fortune 500 manufacturer), expect: multi-round legal and InfoSec review, ERP integration deep-dive (SAP Ariba or Coupa replacement/integration), procurement-led vendor onboarding process, and likely a phased SOW (pilot + rollout). No SI or implementation partner is named in the contact roles or fields. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null, so there is no forcing-function contract renewal deadline to anchor the timeline. The absence of a known expiration date or compliance mandate further weakens the urgency and makes the close date less defensible. If the deal re-engages, the immediate move is to map the contracting and implementation path (legal, InfoSec, IT integration, procurement onboarding) so the timeline can be re-anchored to real milestones.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: three Decision Maker roles (Priscila Coelho, ASHWINI N SHINGOTE) but zero Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor roles

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified or engaged. The contact roles include three Decision Makers (Priscila Coelho, Global Strategic Sourcing Project Manager; ASHWINI N SHINGOTE, VP IT and Global IT Leader for GPO/GSS/EHS/Sustainability) but no Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role. Decision Makers can influence the selection, but the Economic Buyer controls the budget and signs off on the commercial investment. ASHWINI N SHINGOTE's VP IT title suggests potential budget authority, but there is zero evidence of commercial or pricing engagement with this person or anyone else at the C-level. The Next Step update (17 Jun) confirms funding is questionable, which means budget authority has not validated or approved the investment. Without Economic Buyer engagement, the deal cannot progress past vendor selection even if evaluation proceeds. The immediate move is to request an executive connect (the deal owner's manager) with ASHWINI N SHINGOTE or the CFO/CPO to validate budget availability, secure funding approval, and confirm executive sponsorship.

decision_process.narrative: The contact-role composition suggests a cross-functional evaluation committee: IT Procurement (Pravallika Yemba, IT Global Procurement Portfolio Manager; Shane M Hardin, Procurement IT Services; Lionel Tagne Djouguem, Manager IT Procurement; Daniel J Degraphenreed, Sr. IT Sourcing Analyst), Strategic Sourcing (Priscila Coelho, Global Strategic Sourcing Project Manager; Caio A Aggio, Manager Global Procurement Tech/Data/AI Strategy; Kim Klaer, Sr. Manager GSS Center of Excellence), and IT Leadership (ASHWINI N SHINGOTE, VP IT). The breadth suggests a thorough, multi-stakeholder process is planned, but zero execution has occurred. No demo, no RFI, no RFP, no workshop, no commercials discussion in 283 days. The Next Step update (17 Jun) reports the project is delayed and funding is questionable, which means the process has stalled before it started. The approval chain is unknown (SF Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c are null). Without knowing who must approve beyond the Decision Makers, Zycus cannot map the path to close. The immediate move is to re-qualify with Priscila Coelho and ASHWINI N SHINGOTE to confirm: (1) is the evaluation still active, (2) who approves the budget and contract, (3) what is the revised timeline, (4) what milestones must occur before close.

sources: SF Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c: null
Zero formal evaluation milestones in 283 days
Zero Avoma calls (no discovery, demo, or workshop recorded)
decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria have been documented. The buyer has not shared an RFP, BRD, scoring matrix, or use-case requirements. Zero evaluation milestones (demo, RFI round, RFP round, workshop, use-case validation) have been executed in the 283 days since deal creation. The product scope is broad (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) but there is no evidence the buyer has defined what success looks like, how they will score vendors, or what capabilities are must-have vs nice-to-have. Without criteria, Zycus cannot tailor the demo, build the differentiation story, or de-risk evaluation against competitors. The absence of criteria also signals weak buyer intent and unstructured process. The immediate move is to run a discovery call with the Decision Makers (Priscila Coelho, ASHWINI N SHINGOTE) to surface their top 3-5 priorities, map those to Zycus capabilities (Merlin agentic AI for negotiation/intake, AI-powered contract risk analysis, supplier collaboration), and co-build the evaluation framework.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are null, so no AI appetite signal is recorded in Salesforce. The product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA, suggesting Zycus has positioned an AI-forward solution, but without buyer engagement (no calls, no demo) there is zero evidence the buyer has articulated AI needs, evaluated AI capabilities, or expressed AI excitement. The presence of Caio A Aggio (Tech/Data/AI Strategy lead) in the contact roles suggests Whirlpool may have a strategic interest in AI-powered procurement, but this is inferred, not confirmed. The deal requires a value-discovery session with Caio and the Decision Makers to test AI appetite, surface AI use cases (agentic supplier negotiation, contract intelligence, intake automation), and position Merlin as the differentiation wedge.

items.detail: Funding is questionable per Next Step 17 Jun 2026. The champion reported the project is delayed due to funding concerns, and Whirlpool shows a pattern of starting and stopping procurement initiatives. No Economic Buyer is identified or engaged to validate budget availability or secure approval. This is the single largest blocker to advancing the deal and must be resolved before any other milestone.

Whirlpool shows a pattern of starting and stopping procurement initiatives per Next Step 17 Jun 2026, signaling weak executive alignment, competing priorities, or governance instability. Without a clear forcing function (contract expiration, compliance deadline, executive mandate) and active C-level sponsorship, this deal may never progress. Executive alignment must be validated in the re-qualification call.

The deal has been in Qualified stage for ~283 days with no progression to the relevant stakeholder or Shortlisted. Zero formal evaluation milestones (demo, RFI, RFP, workshop) have been executed. The close date of 25 Sep 2026 (81 days out) is not credible given the current pace and the unresolved funding blocker. The timeline is at serious risk and likely requires a forward slip or disqualification if buyer engagement does not restart immediately.

Zero buyer engagement in the evidence window (no calls, no formal evaluation activity) and a cooling champion who reports delays but does not unblock them suggest political risk or loss of internal sponsorship at Whirlpool. The deal may have lost mindshare or been deprioritized. Re-qualification must test whether there is still genuine buyer intent and active internal advocacy.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal shows multiple negative confidence signals: zero buyer engagement (no calls, no emails, no meetings) in the evidence window; passive champion who reports delays but does not unblock; funding is questionable per Next Step 17 Jun; Whirlpool shows a pattern of starting/stopping procurement initiatives; deal stalled in Qualified for 283 days with zero formal evaluation milestones; no Economic Buyer identified or engaged; no quantified value case built; close date 81 days out is not credible. The deal is cooling toward dark and requires immediate re-qualification to test buyer intent. If the buyer does not re-engage within 14 days, the deal should be disqualified or moved to Omitted forecast.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 81 (close 2026-09-25). Time in Qualified stage: ~283 days (created 2025-09-16, still Qualified 2026-07-06). No submission-date series available. Zero forward slip recorded in field history, but the deal has made no stage progression in nine months. LastActivityDate 2026-06-17 (19 days ago) reflects a rep-initiated Next Step update, NOT buyer engagement. No verified two-way buyer touch in the entire evidence window. Required pace to close on time: immediate progression to Formal Evaluation, demo, and RFI/RFP within the next 30 days, then Shortlisted by mid-August and Vendor Selected by early September. Current pace: zero milestones completed in 283 days. The close date is indefensible given the funding blocker, lack of Economic Buyer, and absence of any formal evaluation activity.

north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified deal stalled 283 days on funding uncertainty; no buyer engagement, no formal evaluation milestone executed, close date 81 days out is not credible without immediate re-qualification and Economic Buyer validation.

flags: Re-qualify or disqualify immediately. This deal has been in Qualified stage for ~283 days with zero buyer calls, no formal evaluation milestone (demo, RFI, RFP, workshop), and a funding blocker flagged 9 days ago. The buyer's pattern of starting and stopping procurement initiatives suggests this may never close. Conduct a direct re-qualification call with the relevant stakeholder Coelho and VP IT to determine: Is there genuine executive sponsorship and a forcing function (contract expiration, compliance deadline) that will unlock funding? If not, recommend moving this opportunity to Omitted forecast and freeing capacity for live deals.

Map and engage the Economic Buyer to resolve the funding blocker. The Next Step flags 'questionable funding' but no Economic Buyer is identified or engaged. ASHWINI SHINGOTE (VP IT, Decision Maker role) or a C-level procurement/finance executive likely controls budget. The deal cannot advance without their active sponsorship. Recommend an executive connect (facilitated by the deal owner's manager) to validate budget availability, understand the funding approval chain, and secure commitment. This is the single highest-leverage move to restart momentum.

Build a quantified ROI case tied to buyer-stated pain. Without a value case anchored to measurable pain (procurement cycle time, cost, compliance risk), there is no economic justification to release the questioned funding. The discovery call must extract: What specific problem is Whirlpool trying to solve? What is the current cost or risk of that problem? What business outcome (savings, efficiency, compliance) would justify a $300k S2P investment? Co-author a financial model with the buyer's procurement and finance leads, and tie it to a forcing function that creates urgency.

The deal requires immediate re-qualification to test buyer intent, validate funding, map Economic Buyer, and either restart with a realistic timeline or disqualify.

AIS fields are null, so no AI appetite signal is recorded in Salesforce. If the deal re-engages, the Merlin agentic AI narrative must be the centerpiece of the demo and ROI case.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No formal evaluation criteria or buyer expectations have been documented, so fit cannot be assessed. The broad product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) suggests Zycus can address end-to-end Source-to-Pay needs, including AI-powered contract intelligence (Merlin), agentic negotiation and intake automation, and risk management. However, without buyer-stated priorities, success criteria, or use-case validation, Zycus has not demonstrated fit or differentiation. The absence of a demo or workshop means the buyer has not seen the solution and cannot assess fit. If the deal re-engages, the immediate priority is to run a discovery session to surface the buyer's top 3-5 priorities and then tailor a demo that proves Zycus can deliver against those priorities, with Merlin agentic AI as the differentiation wedge for procurement organizations focused on supplier negotiation leverage, contract risk intelligence, and intake automation.

hard.ais_why:
• Project started in 2024 around Supplier, Sourcing, and Merlin — and grew into a full S2C effort to replace iValua due to gaps in AI, but momentum has now stalled.
• May 13 update is blunt: no change, project delayed per the champion, and the prospect shows a clear start-stop pattern on procurement initiatives.
• Last activity April 21, 2026 — Merlin being in scope keeps it Curious, but the stop-start pattern caps the score.

deltas.to: Unnamed (likely Lionel Tagne Djouguem, Manager IT Procurement) - weak
deltas.subject: champion

Re-qualify or disqualify immediately. This deal has been in Qualified stage for ~283 days with zero buyer calls, no formal evaluation milestone (demo, RFI, RFP, workshop), and a funding blocker flagged 9 days ago. The buyer's pattern of starting and stopping procurement initiatives suggests this may never close. Conduct a direct re-qualification call with the relevant stakeholder Coelho and VP IT to determine: Is there genuine executive sponsorship and a forcing function (contract expiration, compliance deadline) that will unlock funding? If not, recommend moving this opportunity to Omitted forecast and freeing capacity for live deals.

Map and engage the Economic Buyer to resolve the funding blocker. The Next Step flags 'questionable funding' but no Economic Buyer is identified or engaged. ASHWINI SHINGOTE (VP IT, Decision Maker role) or a C-level procurement/finance executive likely controls budget. The deal cannot advance without their active sponsorship. Recommend an executive connect (facilitated by the deal owner's manager) to validate budget availability, understand the funding approval chain, and secure commitment. This is the single highest-leverage move to restart momentum.

Build a quantified ROI case tied to buyer-stated pain. Without a value case anchored to measurable pain (procurement cycle time, cost, compliance risk), there is no economic justification to release the questioned funding. The discovery call must extract: What specific problem is Whirlpool trying to solve? What is the current cost or risk of that problem? What business outcome (savings, efficiency, compliance) would justify a $300k S2P investment? Co-author a financial model with the buyer's procurement and finance leads, and tie it to a forcing function that creates urgency.

Anchor the timeline to a forcing function (FY27 planning, compliance deadline, executive procurement transformation initiative) to overcome the pattern of starting/stopping.

Conduct a direct re-qualification call with the relevant stakeholder Coelho and VP IT to determine: Is there genuine executive sponsorship and a forcing function (contract expiration, compliance deadline) that will unlock funding? If not, recommend moving this opportunity to Omitted forecast and freeing capacity for live deals.

Recommend an executive connect (facilitated by the deal owner's manager) to validate budget availability, understand the funding approval chain, and secure commitment. This is the single highest-leverage move to restart momentum.

value.flag: Build a quantified ROI case tied to buyer-stated pain. Without a value case anchored to measurable pain (procurement cycle time, cost, compliance risk), there is no economic justification to release the questioned funding. The discovery call must extract: What specific problem is Whirlpool trying to solve? What is the current cost or risk of that problem? What business outcome (savings, efficiency, compliance) would justify a $300k S2P investment? Co-author a financial model with the buyer's procurement and finance leads, and tie it to a forcing function that creates urgency.
